cases.notes: User explicitly names a completed todo that exists in the input. Expected: agent emits a single update_todo op flipping the matching todo to done, plus a brief conversational confirmation. No insert_entry needed — routine todo flips don't double-log.

input.utterance: Finished the auth migration on Intently — surprisingly clean once Supabase RLS clicked.

must_surface: matched_project.id is 'P-INTENTLY' (the project containing the matched todo)
updates contains at least one entry with table='projects', op='update_todo', project_id='P-INTENTLY', todo_id='T-AUTH', set.done=true
If an entries.insert is also emitted, its body_markdown is the user's words verbatim (light grammar edits only) and links.project_id='P-INTENTLY'
confirmation is a 1-2 sentence acknowledgement that names the project (Intently) and the completed item (auth migration)
fenced ```json block is the last thing in the response
anti_patterns: Inventing todo_ids that aren't in the input
Flipping a todo on the wrong project (e.g. Design System Port)
Paraphrasing the user's words in body_markdown ('User completed authentication work' instead of their phrasing)
Plan-mode output (proposing next actions, suggesting priorities)
Updating projects.body_markdown or projects.status (out-of-scope for a single todo flip)

cases.notes: User asks a planning question — looks superficially like a tracker context (mentions a project) but is requesting advice, not logging completed work. Expected: agent emits empty updates array with ambiguous=false and a redirect in confirmation. No DB writes should land. This case verifies CR-update-tracker-06 (scope discipline) and the explicit out-of-scope handling in section 3 of the prompt.

input.utterance: What should I work on next for Intently? I'm not sure if I should tackle the demo recording or the auth migration first.

must_surface: ambiguous is false (this isn't a disambiguation case — the utterance is clearly a question, not an update)
confirmation is a brief redirect acknowledging that this is a planning question and not within update-tracker's scope (e.g. 'sounds like a planning question — want me to pull up the brief?')
fenced ```json block is the last thing in the response
anti_patterns: Any updates entry — no DB writes for planning questions
Marking either todo done (the user explicitly hasn't done either yet)
Inserting an entries row capturing the question (this is chat, not a journal)
Recommending which task to do (out of scope — that's daily-brief or chat)
ambiguous=true (this isn't a 'which project did you mean' case)
